ibis.text: What are the long-term societal impacts of COVID-19?
orig: What are the long-term societal impacts of COVID-19?

ibis.text: Policymakers should build multi-level governance structures based on empowering participation, engagement and cooperation to strengthen the capacity to identify and respond to local needs.
orig: Build multi-level governance structures based on empowering participation, engagement and cooperation to strengthen the capacity to identify and respond to local needs.

ibis.text: Tensions and inconsistencies between different levels of governance have affected the coordination and effectiveness of the policy response to COVID-19.
orig: There is strong evidence that tensions and inconsistencies between different levels of governance have affected the coordination and effectiveness of the policy response to COVID-19.

ibis.text: Government should improve the way knowledge, data and information are developed, shared and communicated to enable all decision-makers to work from a shared understanding of the facts.
orig: Improve the way we develop, share and communicate knowledge, data and information to enable all decision-makers to work from shared understanding of the facts.

ibis.text: Coordinated and shared view of the facts is in the public interest to mitigate more effectively the social impacts of this crisis and be better prepared for future crises.
orig: a coordinated and shared view of the facts is in the public interest
to mitigate more effectively the social impacts of this crisis and be better prepared for future crises

ibis.text: Investment in digital infrastructure should be prioritised as a critical public service to eliminate the digital divide and create a more equitable basis for education and employment.
orig: Prioritise investment in digital infrastructure as a critical public service to eliminate the digital divide, improve communication and joint problem solving, and create a more equitable basis for education and employment.

ibis.text: Geographical and socioeconomic inequalities in digital access across the country remain a primary barrier to levelling up.
orig: The existing geographical and socioeconomic inequalities in digital access across the country should be tackled as they remain a primary barrier to levelling up.

ibis.text: Urban spaces should be reimagined to support sustainable and adaptable local businesses, amenities, and lifestyles.
orig: Reimagine urban spaces to support sustainable and adaptable local businesses, amenities and lifestyles.

ibis.text: Urban regeneration will protect society and the environment against unsustainable urban-to-rural migration and suburban sprawl.
orig: It will also protect society and the environment against unsustainable urban-to-rural migration and suburban sprawl

ibis.text: A more agile, responsive education and training system should be created to meet the needs of a new social and economic environment.
orig: Create a more agile, responsive education and training system capable of meeting the needs of a new social and economic environment

ibis.text: The pandemic has exacerbated existing employment trends and disproportionately impacted the vulnerable, requiring a rethinking of the types of knowledge and skills needed.
orig: exacerbate existing employment trends across sectors and geographic communities and disproportionately impact the vulnerable.
These changes will require a rethinking of the types of knowledge and skills needed in a new social and economic environment

ibis.text: Government should strengthen and expand community-led social infrastructure that underpins vital services needed to enhance local resilience.
orig: Strengthen and expand community-led social infrastructure that underpins the vital services and support structures needed to enhance local resilience

ibis.text: Community-led social infrastructure has been an essential but precarious lifeline in the crisis, and its importance will grow as we respond to long-term societal effects.
orig: Community-led social infrastructure has been an essential but precarious lifeline in the crisis, and its importance will only grow as we look to respond to and mitigate the long-term societal effects.

ibis.text: Policymakers should empower a range of actors, including business and civil society, to work together with a sense of social purpose to drive a solid strategy for recovery.
orig: Empower a range of actors, including business and civil society, to work together with a sense of social purpose to help drive a solid strategy for recovery across the economy and society.

ibis.text: Achievements in innovation during the crisis were driven by different actors working together towards a common goal because of a shared sense of urgency and necessity.
orig: different actors worked together towards a common goal because of a shared sense of urgency and necessity.

ibis.text: What kind of policy framework and wider landscape is required to underpin progress towards shaping a positive post-pandemic future?
orig: What does this tell us about the policy framework and wider policy landscape that might underpin progress towards shaping a positive post-pandemic future?

ibis.text: A resilient and effective state should follow five principles: Communicative, Learning, Engaging, Adaptive and Relational.
orig: In addition to the seven strategic policy goals, we conclude that a more resilient and effective state should follow five principles which we label CLEAR: Communicative, Learning, Engaging, Adaptive and Relational.

ibis.text: The CLEAR principles support the delivery of strategic policy goals and provide a framework for a successful recovery by 2030.
orig: These principles can work collectively to support the delivery of the strategic policy goals and be used as a framework for a successful recovery by 2030.
